name: iSPIRT
full_name: Indian Software Product Industry Round Table
description: iSPIRT is India's premier software product think tank and volunteer-driven community. Founded by Sharad Sharma and Mohandas Pai, it has been the backbone of India Stack (UPI, Aadhaar, ONDC) and runs the PlaybookRT sessions — invite-only deep-dives with category-defining founders. Known for policy influence and making India the largest product nation.
value_for_founders: PlaybookRT sessions are among the highest-signal founder interactions in India — small groups of 10-15 founders with a 'volunteer captain' who has solved the exact problem you're facing. iSPIRT's M&A Connect program and SaaSBOOMi collaboration give unparalleled access to India's best operators. Policy access means founders in FinTech and SaaS can shape the regulatory environment.
how_to_join: 1. Apply at ispirt.in/playbooks → fill the PlaybookRT application form describing your specific challenge. 2. Volunteer Captains review and invite relevant founders. 3. Sessions are invite-only and held in-person in 3-4 cities. Start by attending open iSPIRT events to get on the radar.

name: SaaSBOOMi
description: SaaSBOOMi is India's most influential B2B SaaS community, founded by Girish Mathrubootham (Freshworks) and other category-defining founders. It runs peer cohorts, quarterly roundtables, the annual SaaSBOOMi conference, and founder-to-founder mentoring. The community's core belief: 'help each other, compete later.'
value_for_founders: Direct peer access to founders building $10M-$500M ARR SaaS businesses. Quarterly roundtables are structured problem-solving sessions where you get tactical advice from operators who've been exactly where you are. The annual conference draws 2,000+ SaaS founders and is the best conference in India for B2B SaaS networking. The fundraising cohort programs connect founders directly with VCs.
how_to_join: 1. Apply at saasboomi.com → 'Join Community'. 2. Applications reviewed by the SaaSBOOMi board (peers, not staff). 3. Must be building a B2B SaaS product and have at least one paying customer. 4. Attend a SaaSBOOMi Connect event in your city to get visibility.

name: NASSCOM 10K Startups
description: India's largest startup ecosystem initiative, run by NASSCOM — India's IT industry body with 2,000+ member companies including TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and all major MNCs. Provides startup validation, co-working spaces in 7 cities, access to enterprise customer connects, and mentorship.
value_for_founders: The single biggest value is access to NASSCOM's 2,000+ member company network for enterprise pilots and partnerships. If you're building B2B enterprise software, a NASSCOM validation opens doors at CIOs of Fortune 500 Indian subsidiaries. The GovTech initiative connects startups directly with government ministries for digital India projects.
how_to_join: 1. Apply at 10000startups.nasscom.in → submit startup profile. 2. Online application reviewed by NASSCOM team. 3. Typically accepted within 2-4 weeks for tech product startups. 4. Post-acceptance, activate Startup Warehouse access in your city.

name: Product Nation
description: India's leading product management blog and community, run by iSPIRT volunteers. Publishes deep-dive product playbooks, founder interviews, and tactical product advice specific to the Indian market. The community Slack has active discussions among Indian PMs and founders.
value_for_founders: Access to India's deepest product management knowledge base. The annual '1,000 Products' survey tracks product hiring across Indian startups — useful for benchmarking team structures. The Slack community has active job boards and PM hiring channels.
how_to_join: 1. Subscribe at productnation.co. 2. Request access to the Product Nation Slack workspace via the website. 3. Attend Product Nation local meetups in Bengaluru or other cities.

name: TiE Bengaluru
description: The Bengaluru chapter of TiE (The Indus Entrepreneurs), the world's largest entrepreneur network. India's most active TiE chapter with regular events, mentorship, and an annual startup competition. Home to TiEcon Bengaluru, the largest startup summit in South India.
value_for_founders: TiE Bengaluru has the densest network of successful Bengaluru-based founders, angel investors, and VCs in a single community. Charter Members (CMs) include founders who've built $100M+ companies and actively invest. The TiE50 competition gives startups global visibility. Mentorship from CMs is structured and ongoing.
how_to_join: 1. Apply for Associate Membership at tie.org/bengaluru (~₹8,000/year). 2. Attend open TiE events first to get comfortable. 3. Apply to TiE50 startup competition if early-stage for free visibility.

name: TiE Delhi-NCR
description: The Delhi-NCR chapter of TiE, one of India's most active chapters. Strong in government-adjacent businesses, EdTech, and traditional industry digitization. Home to TiEcon Delhi and strong connections to the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) ecosystem.
value_for_founders: Best TiE chapter for startups targeting North India markets or government contracts. Strong B2G (business-to-government) startup support and policy connections. Active angels investing in edtech, agritech, and traditional industry digitization.
how_to_join: Apply for Associate Membership at tie.org/delhi. Annual fee ~₹10,000. Attend open TiE events first.

name: TiE Mumbai
description: Mumbai chapter of TiE, with strong connections to India's financial services ecosystem, media industry, and traditional conglomerates (Tata, Reliance, Mahindra). Home to many FinTech and consumer startup angels who've exited from BSE-listed companies.
value_for_founders: Ideal for startups targeting FinTech, NBFC partnerships, media/entertainment, or wanting connections to large Indian conglomerates. Strong angel investor base from financial services. Access to corporate venture arms of Mumbai-based companies.
how_to_join: Apply at tiemumbai.org for Associate Membership. Annual fee ~₹12,000.

name: Headstart Network
description: India's largest grassroots startup community, operating in 20+ cities. Runs monthly startup meetups, pitch events, and hackathons. Known for being the first community that early-stage founders (even at the idea stage) can join. Volunteers run each city chapter independently.
value_for_founders: Best community for a first-time founder who doesn't yet have VC validation. Monthly meetups in 20+ cities attract 50-200 founders, angels, and startup enthusiasts. A great place to find co-founders, first customers, and early team members. The national pitch competition gives exposure across cities.
how_to_join: 1. Find your city's Headstart chapter at headstart.in. 2. RSVP to the next monthly meetup (free). 3. Attend 2-3 events before approaching the city lead about volunteering.

name: Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM)
description: One of India's best state government startup programs, recognized internationally. KSUM has supported 4,000+ startups across Kerala with funding (KSUM grants up to ₹50 lakh), lab access, and ecosystem support. Kerala has a disproportionately strong deep tech and space tech cluster (ISRO, VSSC presence).
value_for_founders: KSUM offers direct grants (not loans) of ₹5-50 lakh for eligible Kerala-based startups without equity dilution. The KSUM incubators give access to specialized labs (hardware prototyping, biotech). Strong alumni network of Kerala founders in Bengaluru, US, and Middle East — who actively support Kerala-origin startups.
how_to_join: 1. Register at startupmission.in → Create profile. 2. Apply for KSUM Recognition. 3. Apply for incubation at a KSUM-supported incubator in your district. Open applications year-round.

name: T-Hub
description: Asia's largest startup incubation hub, backed by Telangana government. T-Hub connects startups with 200+ corporate innovation programs, international ecosystems (Israel, USA, Singapore, Japan), and government contracts. Located in HITECH City with India's largest startup campus.
value_for_founders: T-Hub's corporate innovation programs are unique in India — they structure paid pilot projects between startups and large corporates (Apollo Hospitals, Mahindra, Microsoft, Google). International connects through T-Hub's relationships with Tel Aviv, Austin, and Singapore startup ecosystems enable global expansion. For deep tech startups, T-Hub's lab infrastructure is best in class in South India.
how_to_join: 1. Apply at t-hub.co → 'Apply for Incubation'. 2. Submit application with product/tech details and team background. 3. Review within 4-6 weeks. Physical presence in Hyderabad required for full incubation.
cost: Co-working fees apply (subsidized for accepted startups)

name: CIIE.CO (IIM Ahmedabad)
full_name: Centre for Innovation, Incubation & Entrepreneurship — IIM Ahmedabad
description: India's premier deep tech and climate tech incubator, backed by IIM Ahmedabad. Known for funding hard science startups that need lab infrastructure. CIIE has pioneered impact investing in India and has portfolio companies in clean energy, medical devices, water, and agriculture.
value_for_founders: CIIE provides early-stage grants (₹5-50L) and seed investments (₹25L-₹2Cr) for deep tech startups specifically. Access to IIM-A's alumni network — considered India's most powerful alumni network for business connections. CIIE's corporate partnerships (Mahindra, IDFC, Infosys Foundation) create pilot opportunities for climate/impact startups.
how_to_join: Apply at ciie.co → 'Apply for Incubation'. Quarterly application cycles. Physical presence in Ahmedabad preferred for incubation.
cost: Equity-based investment (5-10% for seed funding) or grant-based

name: NSRCEL (IIM Bangalore)
full_name: N.S. Raghavan Centre for Entrepreneurial Learning — IIM Bangalore
description: India's oldest business incubator, at IIM Bangalore. Has supported 800+ startups over 20+ years. Known for the Goldman Sachs 10,000 Women program, Women Startup Program, and the NSRCEL Gold community connecting all alumni. Strong in sustainability, social enterprise, and Bengaluru-based SaaS.
value_for_founders: NSRCEL's biggest value is its 800+ company alumni network — many alumni are now running ₹50-500Cr companies and actively invest in or partner with NSRCEL portfolio companies. The IIM-B faculty mentorship in strategy and business model is substantive. The Goldman Sachs Women program is one of India's best programs for women founders with real funding support.
how_to_join: 1. Apply at nsrcel.org → 'Apply for Incubation'. 2. Rolling applications accepted. 3. Interview round for selection. Bengaluru-based strongly preferred for in-person incubation.
cost: Equity-free for grant programs; some cohorts take small equity

name: Antler India Alumni
description: Community of founders who went through Antler India's cohort-based program. Antler India runs 2 cohorts per year in Bengaluru, selecting 25-40 founders per cohort for a 10-week founder residency where they form teams, validate ideas, and receive initial investment of $100K-$200K for 10-15% equity.
value_for_founders: Antler India's global network (40 countries) means portfolio companies get warm intros to Antler's corporate and VC partners worldwide. The cohort model creates incredibly strong founder bonds — Antler companies actively support each other with hiring, customer intros, and investor references. Antler India also helps with follow-on fundraising.
how_to_join: 1. Apply to join an Antler India cohort at antler.co/india. 2. Applications open 3 months before each cohort. 3. 3-stage selection: application review → interviews → 2-day residency assessment. 4. Community access is alumni-only post acceptance.

name: Surge Alumni (Peak XV)
full_name: Surge by Peak XV Partners (formerly Sequoia Surge)
description: Surge is Peak XV Partners' (formerly Sequoia India/SEA) accelerator program — arguably the most prestigious startup program in India and Southeast Asia. 4-month intensive program with $1-2M investment. Alumni community is the most elite early-stage founder network in South and Southeast Asia.
value_for_founders: Surge provides $1-2M at seed for ~20% equity + 4 months of intensive support from Peak XV partners and operators. The Surge network gives lifetime access to the most successful founders in India/SEA for co-investing, partnerships, and hiring. Follow-on funding from Peak XV is more likely for Surge companies. The brand equity of 'Surge company' opens VC doors globally.
how_to_join: Apply at surge.in → 'Apply Now'. Applications reviewed for each cohort (2-3 per year). Highly competitive — ~1% acceptance rate. Proof of early traction significantly helps.

name: 100X.VC Portfolio Community
description: 100X.VC is India's most active pre-seed VC, investing in 50+ startups annually via standardized iSAFE notes (India's SAFE equivalent). The portfolio community across 200+ companies is a strong peer network for early-stage founders facing similar challenges of 0-to-1 building.
value_for_founders: 100X.VC invests quickly (decision in 2 weeks) with standardized terms — no negotiation required. The portfolio WhatsApp groups and quarterly events create a support network of 200+ founders all at roughly similar stages. Ninad Karpe and Sanjay Mehta are active in connecting portfolio companies with customer pilots.
how_to_join: Apply for investment at 100x.vc → submit iSAFE application. If funded, portfolio community access is automatic. Also attend 100X.VC events in Mumbai/Bengaluru to network with portfolio founders.
cost: 100X takes equity via iSAFE (standard terms, check size ₹25-50L)

name: Inc42 Community
description: India's leading startup media platform and community. Inc42 covers Indian startup news, funding rounds, policy, and founder stories. The Inc42 Plus community gives access to deep-dive reports, sector analysis, and founder roundtables.
value_for_founders: The Inc42 Plus community gives access to 500+ India startup reports, funding databases, and sector analysis. Getting featured on Inc42 (startup profile or guest article) builds credibility with Indian investors and customers. The Inc42 BrandLab helps startups with PR strategy.
how_to_join: Free signup at inc42.com. Inc42 Plus subscription at ₹4,999/year for premium reports and community access.

name: YourStory Startup Network
description: India's most-read startup publication, founded by Shradha Sharma. YourStory has told the story of over 100,000 startups and runs TechSparks — India's largest annual tech conference. YourStory HerStory covers women founders. The YourStory community focuses on grassroots and Tier-2/Tier-3 startup ecosystems.
value_for_founders: A YourStory feature (1,000-3,000 word founder story) is the most accessible startup PR in India. Many angel investors and customers discover startups through YourStory. TechSparks has the widest brand recognition across India — 30,000+ attendees in Bengaluru. The YourStory SMB community is excellent for founders targeting India's 63 million SMBs.
how_to_join: 1. Submit your startup at yourstory.com/submit-startup for a profile. 2. Apply to speak at TechSparks. 3. Contribute a guest post on YourStory to build community presence.

name: FinTech Association for Consumer Empowerment (FACE)
description: India's leading FinTech industry body, representing 100+ FinTech companies before the RBI, SEBI, IRDAI, and other regulators. FACE is the go-to body for FinTech startups navigating India's complex financial regulation. Has a direct communication channel with the RBI FinTech department.
value_for_founders: FACE membership gives FinTech startups a voice in shaping regulation — participating in FACE's consultations with RBI has influenced NBFC licensing norms, digital lending guidelines, and payment regulations. The FACE network connects startups with established NBFCs and banks for partnership/lending agreements. Legal and regulatory advisories save months of CA/lawyer fees.
how_to_join: Apply for membership at facets.org.in. Membership fee ₹25,000-₹50,000/year depending on startup stage. Recommendation from an existing member helps.

name: Startup India Hub
full_name: Startup India Hub (DPIIT / Government of India)
description: The official Government of India startup platform under DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade). Startupindia.gov.in is the gateway for DPIIT Recognition, scheme discovery, investor-startup matchmaking, and mentor connect. The Hub hosts India's largest startup database.
value_for_founders: The Startup India Hub is mandatory infrastructure — your DPIIT Recognition certificate (obtained here) unlocks all 47+ government schemes. The Startup India Investor Connect module lists 500+ SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) looking for startups. The mentor database has 1,200+ mentors. Free webinars on legal, IP, and regulatory topics.
how_to_join: Register free at startupindia.gov.in. Apply for DPIIT Recognition (takes 2-7 days, free). Then activate benefits as needed.

name: Women in Tech India
description: India's largest community for women in technology and entrepreneurship. Runs city-level chapters, mentorship programs, and an annual conference. Actively supports women founders with investor introductions, peer support, and leadership development.
value_for_founders: The WiT India network provides women founders with access to a curated network of 500+ women investors, VCs with diversity mandates, and corporations with women-supplier programs. The NASSCOM partnership gives access to women founder grants. Peer mentoring from successful women founders across all stages.
how_to_join: Register at witindia.org or join the city-specific Slack/WhatsApp group. Most events are free.
annual_events: Women in Tech India Annual Conference

name: DeepTech Club India
description: India's emerging community for deep tech founders, built from BIRAC alumni, IIT research spin-off founders, and ISRO/DRDO entrepreneurs. Focus on hardware, biotech, advanced materials, space, and AI research commercialization. Strong connection to India's national research laboratories.
value_for_founders: The DeepTech Club is the best community in India for founders commercializing research from IITs, IISc, CSIR labs, and DRDO. Connects hardware founders with prototype labs, BIRAC grant experts, and deeptech-focused VCs (Chiratae, Pi Ventures, Speciale Invest). The community helps navigate India's complex IP regime and technology transfer from academic institutions.
how_to_join: Join the DeepTech Club India LinkedIn group and Slack community. Attend city-level meetups in Bengaluru or Hyderabad. Apply for the annual cohort program.
